expertAssessments.rationale: GitLab main validate, tag deploy pipeline, production /api/version, Cloud Run /health, and usage guardrails all passed for v8.12.74.
The deployed AI Engine answered a direct server-id CPU comparison through deterministic monitoring-metric-current evidence with no LLM fallback.
Cloud Run health and usage checks confirm the deployed monitoring AI path is live and remains within configured guardrails.

usageChecks.summary: v8.12.74 production deploy after check: effective 18.5833 USD, billed 0.0000 USD, chargeCount 16443.
Cloud Run guardrails intact: maxScale=1, concurrency=16, timeout=300s, cpu=1, memory=512Mi, cpu-throttling=true, latestReadyRevision=ai-engine-00579-hvn.

artifacts.label: v8.12.74 release smoke command evidence
artifacts.note: Structured release smoke evidence from code review, local validation, GitLab pipelines, production /api/version, Cloud Run /health, direct AI smoke, and usage guard checks.

notes: This run records deployment propagation and smoke evidence only; it intentionally does not count toward aggregate QA summary totals.
The first cold-cache Docker preflight timed out during build, then the warm-cache rerun passed before release.
The release readiness helper printed GitLab token not found/manual checklist mode, but the GitLab main and tag pipelines were verified separately and both succeeded.
The root artifact audit caught misplaced QA screenshots before push; cleanup moved them under tmp/root-artifacts and the strict audit passed.
The moderate ws advisory remains non-blocking dependency audit debt and was not fixed by this release.
